rulings.Case Title: HERNANDEZ, VIRGILIO MUNGIA vs. CERDA, MARTIN SANTOS IND DBA SANTOS TRUCKING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, may 25, 2012, Line 1, plantiff virgilio hernandez' motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Moving party to provide original of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID MELANSON VS. S.J. AMOROSO, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 25, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANT LVI EVNIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant fails to establish that the completed and accepted doctrine applies because the evidence indicates that defendant was still working on the third floor, the scene of the accident, after the injury. (Chance v. Lawyer's Inc. (1962) 58 Cal.2d 368). There is a triable issue whether the hazard was open and obvious and whether plaintiff acted unreasonably when placing his ladder near the depression. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY L. TYLER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 25, 2012, Line 7. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the dangerous condition of public property claim and and denied as to the negligence cause of action. As to the negligence cause of action, Defendants did not satisfy their burden of production. Defendants fail to show that Mr. Briones did not breach the standard of care. Ms. Ochoa testified at 20:12-22:11 of her deposition that she saw plaintiff prior to Mr. Briones slowing down. The reasonable inference is that Mr. Briones breached the standard of care by proceeding through the intersection without seeing or paying attention to plaintiff. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of cause of action for dangerous condition of public property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition For An Order Compelling Arbitreation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 25, 2012, Line 12. PETITIONER DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition For An Order Compelling Arbitration. Granted. Because Mr. Montiel did not seek mediation within the time frame required by the parties' contract, the parties' disputes are to be arbitrated. Per Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6, the court nominates the following five persons, all of who are affiliated with the San Francisco office of ADR Services: 1) Ina Gyemant; 2) Everett Hewlett; 3) Michael McCabe; 4) Bonnie Sabraw; and 5) Alex Saldamando. The parties are required to send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 5pm June 4, 2012 stating whether or not they agreed on an arbitrator, and, if they did not, the court will select the arbitrator from one of the nominees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GCFS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MARYANN CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Withdraw Or Amend Facts And Documents Deemed Admitted By Court Order Dated Apr 6, 2012; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, may 25, 2012, Line 6, defendant maryann chiu's Motion To Withdraw Or Amend Facts And Documents Deemed Admitted By Court Order Dated Apr 6, 2012. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
